body.title: One central inference proxy; Hex is the first bottleneck; saturation-escalation is the relief valve
body.text:
# D-single-central-inference-proxy-2026-07-07
**date:** 2026-07-07
**status:** open

## Decision (Akien)
There is ONE central inference proxy (the limited Ollama HTTP door in the inference shim, T-aider-through-inference-proxy), NOT per-box. Every aider instance in the swarm (Aider.0 here, Aider.1 on Windows, ...) points AIDER_PROXY_BASE at the single proxy over the network. Rationale: one budget-ledger, one io_corpus, one routing policy -> swarm-wide cost is ONE measurable thing and escalation is uniform. The proxy is a thin http.server router (I/O-bound, negligible) so it will not bottleneck for a long time. Split to per-box or multiple proxies ONLY if the proxy itself is ever proven the constraint.

## Insight: Hex bottlenecks first
The compute (Hex = M1 Max 32GB running 24-30B models) saturates long before the proxy does. As the swarm scales (N aider builders -> 1 proxy -> Hex), the capacity limit is Hex throughput.

## Consequence + the real lever
The central proxy's cloud-escalation is the RELIEF VALVE for Hex saturation: overflow routes to Ollama-cloud/OpenRouter by policy, and the ledger measures the Hex-vs-cloud cost tradeoff. BUT a bottlenecked Hex goes SLOW/QUEUED, not DOWN — and today tier-escalation fires on source FAILURE/unavailability, not SATURATION. So the valve as built won't trip on a busy-but-up Hex; it just waits. The real lever = SATURATION-AWARE routing: escalate when Hex latency (p95) or queue-depth crosses a threshold, not only on failure. FOLLOW-UP (not yet built): latency/queue-depth-aware escalation in the inference router.

## Links
Builds on T-aider-through-inference-proxy (shipped). Feeds T-aider-swarm-deployment (per-box aider identity; all point at the one proxy). Relates to project_inference_cost_optimizing_router, project_hex_ollama_model_slate, project_mac_studio_inference_host.

## Intention
I intend one central proxy so swarm inference cost + routing are one measurable thing, and that Hex saturation (not just failure) becomes an escalation trigger so the cloud relief valve actually trips under swarm load.
